handoff_goal: Specify workflow phase skills as recommended bindings to existing skills, including shared-catalog authoring, dependency-aware import/export, active-phase prompt behavior, and lightweight runtime evidence.

current_project_roles.scope: Stores this sp-discussion session and durable artifacts.
current_project_roles.notes: The active repository is the discussion host.

current_project_roles.scope: Overall cc-jiangxia workflows product and agent capability direction.
current_project_roles.evidence_source: user selected a fresh overall workflow direction
current_project_roles.notes: Discussion is not resuming prior handoff-ready workflow discussions.

target_project_roles.scope: cc-jiangxia workflow template, runtime, import/export, skill catalog, and desktop workflow authoring surfaces.
target_project_roles.evidence_source: user direction plus live reads in project-context.md
target_project_roles.notes: Current project cognition was stale/blocked; live repository evidence is authoritative.

target_paths_still_to_verify: Whether a new shared skill catalog service should be extracted from src/server/api/skills.ts and src/skills/loadSkillsDir.ts.
Whether active workflow runtime evidence belongs in session state, phase run records, final report records, or all three.
implementation_target.cross_project_cognition_rule: Current project cognition cannot prove another project's implementation facts; this handoff targets the current project only.

source_evidence.claim: Matching skills are a blocking requirement for the agent to consider before responding.
Existing skills can be invoked by name and may affect allowed tools, model, effort, inline context, or forked execution.
Runtime skill sources include broader origins than current workflow template skill validation, including plugin, managed, bundled, and MCP.
Current WorkflowSkillDeclaration is prompt guidance from templates, not an executable skill binding.
Runtime prompt assembly currently renders workflow skill guidance as guidance.
Current policy explicitly says workflow skill declarations do not enable SkillTool globally.
Current phase skills normalization allows objects with name, limited source values, optional reason, and unknown fields.
Current workflow export exports template JSON and does not bundle skill package contents.
Settings > Skills is backed by a skills API and desktop skill store, but current server collection primarily covers user/project/plugin skills.
Phase skills are currently edited through an advanced freeform textarea and converted line-by-line into skill declarations.
User confirmed phase skills should bind to skills, not plugins; plugin identity is provenance/dependency only.
User confirmed import-with-warnings for missing recommended phase skills.
User confirmed UI mainline: phase-local selector, import/export diagnostics, lightweight runtime status.

soft_unknowns.claim: Exact phase skill binding schema fields remain to be specified.
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if schema details require changing confirmed recommended-first semantics.

soft_unknowns.claim: Exact shared catalog service/resolver boundary remains to be specified.
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if workflow authoring would use a separate inventory from Settings/runtime.

soft_unknowns.claim: Exact runtime evidence storage and report shape remain to be specified.
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if evidence cannot survive resume/compaction.

soft_unknowns.claim: Future required/contract phase skill behavior is intentionally out of first scope.
soft_unknowns.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if required skills are pulled into first scope.

settled_decisions: Workflow phase skills are recommended bindings to existing skills by default.
Workflow phases bind to skills, not plugins.
Plugin identity is provenance/dependency for plugin-provided skills.
Phase skills are selected from a shared Settings-backed skill catalog.
Recommended phase skills are not auto-executed and do not block completion.
Importing workflows with missing recommended phase skills is allowed with warnings and degraded state.
Workflow export includes dependency manifest and does not bundle skill package contents by default.
Primary UI is phase-local skill selection in WorkflowTemplateEditor.
assumptions_to_preserve: Current project cognition is advisory only because it was stale/blocked.
Existing unknown-field preservation should be maintained.
Existing reason fields may exist but must not become the new source of skill applicability.
conflicts_requiring_return_to_discussion: Any design that auto-executes recommended skills by default.
Any design that makes recommended skills block import or phase completion by default.
Any design that binds workflows to plugins instead of skills.
Any design that silently drops missing skill references.
Any design that bypasses SkillTool permission/safety boundaries.
recommended_sequence: Specify schema and source resolution semantics.
Specify shared catalog and import/export dependency diagnostics.
Specify desktop authoring and status surfaces.
Plan implementation and verification by surface.
reopen_conditions: Confirmed semantics cannot be implemented without weakening safety boundaries.
Shared catalog cannot represent the sources needed for workflow authoring.
Import/export cannot preserve missing skill references.

confirmed_ui_decisions: Phase skill selection belongs in the selected phase area of WorkflowTemplateEditor.
The selector should use the shared skill catalog, grouped by source.
Selected skills should show name, source, availability, and plugin provenance when relevant.
Import/export should show workflow-level skill dependency diagnostics.
Runtime workflow status should show recommended skill status only as a concise strip or expandable evidence section.
UI should avoid full checklist behavior for every recommended skill.
deferred_ui_decisions: Whether runtime status is always visible or expandable by default.
interaction_expectations: Add skill opens searchable shared catalog.
Phase-local selected skills are editable without touching other phases.
Dependency diagnostics are visible before import commit and before export sharing.
accessibility_expectations: Selector and chips must be keyboard accessible.
Warnings must be screen-reader visible text, not color-only.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Workflow phase skills should become real recommended bindings to existing skills, not prompt-only workflow guidance.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must define phase skill as a first-class binding concept over existing skills.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if phase skills are represented only as prose guidance.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: First scope targets recommended phase skills, shared-catalog authoring, dependency-aware import/export, active-phase prompt emphasis, and lightweight runtime evidence.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Keep scope unified and do not expand into full workflow engine scheduling.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if scope expands to auto-execution or workflow engine orchestration by default.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Recommended phase skills must not auto-execute or block phase completion by default.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must reserve blocking behavior for future required/contract mode.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if recommended skills become mandatory completion gates.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Workflow phase bindings target skills, not plugins; plugin identity is provenance/dependency for plugin-provided skills.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Schema and UI must store skill identity first and plugin metadata second.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if workflow templates bind to plugins as the primary object.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Phase skill selection comes from the shared capability catalog behind Settings > Skills and plugin capability navigation.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must define or align a shared catalog/resolver consumed by settings, workflow authoring, import/export, and runtime.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if workflow authoring uses a separate skill inventory.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Skill references are names-first; source/qualified metadata is added only when ambiguity or portability requires it.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Schema should keep authoring simple while preserving disambiguation fields.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if schema requires verbose fully-qualified references for all skills without need.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: The phase binding must not duplicate skill-owned description, reason, or appliesWhen semantics.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: New UI/schema should not copy skill instructions into workflow templates; existing reason must not become the canonical trigger source.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if templates duplicate skill descriptions/triggers as source of truth.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Active phase prompt semantics: selected skills are for this phase; pay special attention; invoke when current task matches; do not invoke when irrelevant.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Runtime prompt assembly must include this behavior without overriding user/system/developer/safety rules.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if prompt semantics are weaker than normal guidance or stronger than mandatory invocation.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Soft audit records used recommended skills and clearly relevant skipped/unavailable skills only.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Completion/report design must avoid exhaustive noisy checklists.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if every recommended skill is mechanically listed as not relevant.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Workflow sharing exports template plus skill dependency manifest and does not bundle skill package contents by default.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Export/import spec must include dependency metadata and avoid copying arbitrary skill files.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if export silently implies skills are bundled.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Missing recommended phase skills allow import with warnings; references are preserved and marked unavailable.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Import preview and runtime must show degraded state without blocking import by default.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if missing recommended skills are silently dropped or block import.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: UI first scope is a phase-local skill selector in WorkflowTemplateEditor.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Desktop spec should replace or augment the advanced skills textarea with a shared-catalog selector.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if authoring moves to only a workflow-level dependency page.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Import/export surfaces show dependency diagnostics as supporting UI.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if imported workflows hide dependency problems.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Runtime UI should show lightweight recommended skill status/evidence only when useful.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Active workflow status should avoid full checklist UX.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if runtime UI becomes noisy or hides unavailable relevant skills.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Skill-derived permissions, shell behavior, hooks, forked agents, model changes, and effort changes must not bypass existing permission/safety boundaries.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec and plan must keep SkillTool permission semantics explicit.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if workflows can indirectly grant tools or run skill effects without normal checks.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Future explicit bundle mode may exist only for reviewed project-owned skills with visible file list/security review.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Keep bundling out of default first scope.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if first scope tries to bundle user/plugin/MCP skill package contents.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Exact schema fields, catalog coverage, and runtime evidence storage are unresolved implementation details, not product blockers.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Resolve during specification/planning without changing confirmed behavior.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if these details require changing confirmed product semantics.

senior_consequence_analysis.trigger_reason: Workflow-declared executable or higher-priority skills can affect agent instruction priority, tool permissions, skill invocation, workflow phase lifecycle, running and resumed sessions, template validation, compaction/resume, and user trust boundaries.
affected_object_map: Skill catalog entries from user, project, plugin, managed, bundled, and MCP sources.
Skill metadata including allowed tools, model, effort, context=fork, agent, hooks, shell expansion, user invocability, and disable-model-invocation.
SkillTool invocation records and context modifiers.
Workflow templates and phase skill declarations.
Workflow template validation, registry writes, unknown-field preservation, import/export wrappers, and desktop authoring APIs.
Workflow session state, phase runs, template snapshots, active prompts, final reports, and resume/stale/missing-template states.
Desktop Settings > Skills, Plugin Detail capability navigation, Workflow Template Editor, Workflow Import/Export Dialog, Workflow Manager, and Workflow Status Panel.

state_behavior_matrix.created: Resolve selected phase skill references and record diagnostics before phase work starts.
state_behavior_matrix.running: Surface recommended skills in active phase prompt; mark unavailable skills explicitly.
state_behavior_matrix.pending-confirmation: Preserve used/skipped/unavailable evidence before transition approval.
state_behavior_matrix.failed: Distinguish workflow failure from skill resolution/execution failure.
state_behavior_matrix.cancelled: Do not retry skill resolution/execution automatically.
state_behavior_matrix.completed: Final report preserves relevant phase skill evidence.
state_behavior_matrix.resumed: Use session snapshot/provenance and re-resolve carefully; do not silently change behavior from edited skill definitions.
state_behavior_matrix.stale-template: Avoid replacing session skill bindings with newer template bindings without a defined policy.
state_behavior_matrix.missing-template: Preserve snapshot skill bindings if available and report missing source template separately.

dependency_impact_table.impact: Invocation, permissions, forked behavior, model/effort context modifiers, audit.
Actual runtime skill sources and metadata.
Current Settings skill catalog API; likely needs shared resolver extraction or alignment.
Template skill reference validation and normalization.
Active phase prompt assembly and runtime evidence.

recovery_and_validation_contract: Add stable schema and migration/compatibility handling before changing authoring UI.
Validate skill references without requiring missing recommended skills to block import.
Preserve unresolved references in templates and session snapshots.
Record resolution status and soft audit evidence in durable state/report paths.
Keep permission prompts explicit for skill-derived tools, shell behavior, hooks, forked agents, model changes, and effort changes.
Provide rollback by preserving old template fields and unknown fields.
Verify with unit tests, server request-shape tests, desktop component tests, and targeted workflow runtime tests.

coverage_gaps.stop_and_reopen_condition: Live code evidence conflicts with handoff assumptions.
coverage_gaps.routing_decision: current workflow may continue with live evidence

coverage_gaps.gap: Current Settings skill API and runtime skill loader may not expose identical source universes.
coverage_gaps.stop_and_reopen_condition: Shared catalog cannot support confirmed authoring/import behavior.

coverage_gaps.gap: Exact storage location for skill resolution/evidence is not selected.

consequence_obligations.claim: Define stable workflow skill binding identity across local, bundled, plugin, managed, and MCP sources.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: If a spec lacks source/version semantics.

consequence_obligations.claim: Define priority precedence without overriding system, developer, security, or explicit user safety boundaries.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: If higher priority implies unsafe override.

consequence_obligations.claim: Define behavior for missing, stale, disabled, invalid, or unavailable workflow skills.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: If missing skills silently degrade to plain prompt text.

consequence_obligations.claim: Preserve explicit permission handling for skill-derived tools, shell expansion, hooks, forked agents, model changes, and effort changes.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: If workflow skill execution bypasses existing SkillTool permission semantics.

consequence_obligations.claim: Snapshot or preserve workflow skill provenance for running, pending, completed, resumed, stale-template, and missing-template sessions.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: If source skill edits can silently change an active session.

consequence_obligations.claim: Make workflow skill execution or non-use observable to the user and downstream artifacts.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: If relevant skill use/skip/unavailability is invisible.

consequence_obligations.claim: Validate workflow skill bindings in workflow template authoring and import/export paths.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: If invalid references persist without diagnostics.

consequence_obligations.claim: Define UI requirements for authoring and inspecting workflow skill bindings.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: If UI state labels for missing/unavailable/used/skipped are absent.

consequence_obligations.claim: Preserve workflow skill evidence through compaction and resume.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: If compaction drops evidence required for later phase decisions.

consequence_obligations.claim: Prevent recursive or unbounded nested skill invocation.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: If auto-invocation or required invocation is introduced without depth, loop, or retry limits.
